data.task:
Ticket 0062, ATOM 1 of a multi-atom priority — establish the DETERMINISTIC core composition contract that lets a priority carry a detailed markdown body after its Objective, preserved verbatim (no summarization). This atom is core-only; the CLI/Play surfaces come in later atoms. DO NOT touch the CLI, daemon, or Play files in this atom.

WRITE SCOPE (stay strictly inside):
- packages/core/src/priorities/**
- packages/core/tests/**
- packages/core/src/index.ts (only to export a new symbol if you add one)

WHAT TO BUILD:
The single owner of the priority markdown format today is `composePriorityMarkdown({id,title,goal})` in packages/core/src/priorities/compose.ts — it wraps a pre-composed `goal` body in frontmatter. Currently the create-priority Play hand-composes the `## Objective` body. We need a deterministic, unit-tested way to build that body from a founder-approved `objective` string plus an OPTIONAL detailed markdown `details` string, so later atoms (CLI --details-file/--details-stdin, edit-priority) can guarantee the details land after the Objective verbatim.

Add a pure function in packages/core/src/priorities/compose.ts (export it from packages/core/src/index.ts):

  composePriorityBody({ objective, details }): string

Behavior:
- Always emit a real `## Objective` section: the heading line `## Objective`, a blank line, then the trimmed `objective` text.
- When `details` is provided and non-empty (after trimming): append a blank line then the details content VERBATIM (no reformatting, no re-wrapping, no summarizing — only normalize surrounding whitespace so there is exactly one blank line between the Objective text and the details block, and ensure the body does not start/end with stray blank lines). Markdown headings inside details (e.g. `## Phase A`, `## Non-goals`) must be preserved exactly.
- When `details` is absent/empty: the body is just the Objective section (this is the current behavior — backward compatible).
- `composePriorityMarkdown` remains the single owner of the frontmatter+body wrapping; do NOT create a second priority template. A natural composition is `composePriorityMarkdown({id, title, goal: composePriorityBody({objective, details})})`. Keep the existing `composePriorityMarkdown({id,title,goal})` signature and all current callers working unchanged.

KEY CORRECTNESS CONSTRAINT (verify against the loader): packages/core/src/priorities/loader.ts `parseObjective` extracts the Objective section up to the NEXT `#`/`##` heading. Therefore details that begin with their own `##`/`#` heading keep `loadPriority(...).objective` equal to ONLY the approved objective text. Your tests must prove this round-trip: a composed priority with a rich details body round-trips through `loadPriority` such that `.objective` === the approved objective (NOT polluted by details) and `.goal` contains the details verbatim.

TESTS (add to packages/core/tests/priorities.test.ts, match the existing style in that file):
- composePriorityBody with objective + multi-section details (include headings like `## Phase A`, `## Phase B`, `## Non-goals`, exact badge text, a verification-gate bullet) → compose to full markdown via composePriorityMarkdown, write to a temp dir, loadPriority round-trips: `.objective` equals exactly the approved objective string, `.goal` contains every details section verbatim.
- composePriorityBody with NO details → body is exactly the Objective section; round-trips with objective non-null; behavior identical to the current Objective-only path.
- details verbatim preservation: assert the composed string contains the details substring byte-for-byte (no summarization/reordering).
- objective is trimmed; leading/trailing blank lines in details do not produce double blank lines or a trailing blank-line pile-up in the body.

MUST NOT BREAK: the existing `composePriorityMarkdown emits loadable priority markdown` test and all existing priorities.test.ts cases must still pass unchanged.

EXIT CRITERION (scripted): from the repo root, `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core test` (or the repo's core test command — check packages/core/package.json scripts) passes, including your new cases, and `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core typecheck` (or repo tsc) is clean. Report the exact commands you ran and their output. Do not run git.

data.reason: composePriorityBody added+exported in core; deterministic verbatim details after trimmed Objective; core typecheck clean and 610 tests pass incl. 4 new round-trip/verbatim/whitespace cases proving loadPriority.objective stays clean.

data.task:
Ticket 0062, ATOM 2 of 5 — add the detailed-body INPUT CHANNEL to the `cocoder oz create-priority` CLI surface. This is the root-cause fix: today the invocation only carries {id,title,objective}, so a founder's detailed Phase A/B/C body never reaches the create-priority Play at all. The daemon already passes the invocation object VERBATIM into the Play prompt (packages/daemon/src/routes.ts:291 — confirmed), so adding a `details` field to the invocation flows it through with NO daemon change. Atom 1 already shipped the deterministic core composer `composePriorityBody({objective,details})`; the Play prompt that consumes `details` is a LATER atom — do NOT touch Plays or the daemon here.

WRITE SCOPE (strictly inside):
- packages/cli/src/oz-args.ts
- packages/cli/src/run.ts
- packages/cli/tests/oz-args.test.ts

WHAT TO BUILD:
1. Extend `createPriorityInvocation` in packages/cli/src/oz-args.ts to accept two NEW optional flags for carrying a detailed markdown body after the Objective:
   - `--details-file <path>` : read the markdown body from a file.
   - `--details-stdin`        : read the markdown body from piped stdin.
   Keep the existing required `--id`, `--title`, `--objective` behavior and error message exactly as-is. When a details source is present and resolves to non-empty content, include it in the returned invocation as a `details` field: `{ id, title, objective, details }`. When no details flag is given, return exactly the current shape `{ id, title, objective }` (NO `details` key) — backward compatible.
2. KEEP oz-args.ts UNIT-TESTABLE WITHOUT REAL I/O. The file header states it is a pure flag→invocation mapping tested without spawning the bin — preserve that. Do the actual file/stdin reading via INJECTED reader dependencies, e.g. give `createPriorityInvocation(args, deps?)` an optional second param like `{ readFileText?: (path: string) => string; readStdin?: () => string }`, defaulting to undefined; the pure parser resolves `details` through those injected readers so tests pass fake readers and never hit the filesystem. (If you prefer a cleaner split, you may instead have oz-args return the parsed flag intent including `detailsFile?`/`detailsStdin?` and resolve content in run.ts — but then the file/stdin RESOLUTION + rejection logic must still be unit-tested somewhere in packages/cli/tests. Pick the more elegant of the two; do not leave the resolution untested.)
3. MALFORMED-INPUT REJECTION (throw a clear Error, exactly like the existing missing-flag path so run.ts prints usage and exits 2):
   - `--details-file` and `--details-stdin` given together → reject (mutually exclusive).
   - `--details-file` with no path value → reject.
   - details source present but resolved content is empty/whitespace-only → reject (a details flag implies real content).
   - file read failure (not found / unreadable) → surface a clear error.
   Do NOT trim away the interior of the details body; only treat all-whitespace as empty for the rejection check. Pass the details content through verbatim (the core composer trims the outer edges).
4. Wire run.ts (the create-priority command block around packages/cli/src/run.ts:188-212): pass REAL readers to `createPriorityInvocation` — `readFileText` via node fs `readFileSync(path,'utf8')`, `readStdin` via a synchronous stdin read (e.g. `readFileSync(0,'utf8')`). The resulting invocation (now possibly carrying `details`) is passed UNCHANGED to `authoringPlayViaDaemon(...)` — no other run.ts logic changes.
5. Update the usage string at packages/cli/src/run.ts:36 to document the new optional flags, e.g.: `usage: cocoder oz create-priority --id <id> --title <text> --objective <text> [--details-file <path> | --details-stdin]`.

TESTS (packages/cli/tests/oz-args.test.ts, match the existing terse style):
- existing required-flag and trimming cases still pass.
- `--details-file` with an injected reader returning multi-section markdown (include `## Phase A`, `## Non-goals`, exact badge text) → invocation.details equals that content verbatim.
- `--details-stdin` with an injected stdin reader → invocation.details equals the piped content verbatim.
- no details flag → returned object has no `details` key (assert backward-compatible shape).
- rejection: both flags together throws; `--details-file` missing path throws; empty/whitespace details throws; reader that throws (missing file) surfaces an error.

MUST NOT BREAK: existing packages/cli tests; the daemon/Play paths (untouched). Do not add a `details` allow-list anywhere in the daemon — it already forwards arbitrary invocation fields.

EXIT CRITERION (scripted): from repo root, `pnpm --filter @cocoder/cli test` and `pnpm --filter @cocoder/cli typecheck` both clean, including your new cases. Report the exact commands and their output. Do not run git.

data.reason: create-priority CLI gains --details-file/--details-stdin via injected readers, carrying details verbatim onto the invocation (no key when absent); mutual-exclusive/missing-path/empty/read-failure all rejected; usage updated; cli typecheck clean and 24 tests pass incl. 8 new.

data.task:
Ticket 0062, ATOM 3 of 5 — make the create-priority AND edit-priority authoring Play prompts CONSUME the detailed-body input and place it VERBATIM after the Objective, using the core composers as the single owners of the format. These are base-governance Plays under packages/personas/base/plays/** edited inside a verified run (the correct verified path for base-Play changes). The CLI now puts a `details` field on the create-priority invocation (atom 2); the edit-priority CLI wrapper is a LATER atom — this atom only teaches the Play prompts and pins the contract with a test. Do NOT touch the CLI, daemon, or run.ts here.

WRITE SCOPE (strictly inside):
- packages/personas/base/plays/create-priority.md
- packages/personas/base/plays/edit-priority.md
- packages/core/tests/orchestration-contracts.test.ts
- packages/core/tests/priority-authoring-plays.test.ts

CONTEXT: both Plays are executionModel: prompt-only (a headless LLM reads the Play markdown + the JSON invocation and writes the priority file itself). Atom 1 shipped core `composePriorityBody({objective, details})` — the deterministic single owner of the priority BODY format (a `## Objective` section, then the details markdown verbatim). `composePriorityMarkdown({id,title,goal})` remains the single owner of the frontmatter+body wrapping. `loadPriority`'s `parseObjective` stops at the next `#`/`##` heading, so detail sections that start with their own heading keep the parsed Objective clean.

=== create-priority.md changes ===
1. In the validation step, note that the founder-approved invocation may carry an OPTIONAL `details` field: founder-approved markdown to place after the Objective. State plainly: when `details` is present it MUST be reproduced VERBATIM — do not summarize, re-wrap, reorder, retitle, or drop any section, badge text, phase, gate, or non-goal. Open research questions inside details are fine (they become the first gate), same as today.
2. Update the compose step (currently step 4, which says 'Compose the priority file using core `composePriorityMarkdown(input)`'): compose the BODY with core `composePriorityBody({ objective, details })` — the single owner of the priority body format — then wrap it with core `composePriorityMarkdown({ id, title, goal })` — the single owner of the frontmatter+body format. KEEP the literal token `composePriorityMarkdown` in the Play text (a contract test asserts it) and ADD the literal token `composePriorityBody`. Do NOT inline or restate either function's output format in prose (the contract test forbids restating the frontmatter/body layout). When `details` is absent, behavior is unchanged (Objective-only body).
3. Keep the round-trip validation step: the written file must round-trip through core `loadPriority`/`readPriorities` with `objective` non-null and, when details were provided, the details content present in the body.
4. Preserve every other existing step (id/collision check, conflict/overlap halt, elegance checkpoint, no-git) unchanged. Keep the 'elegance checkpoint' phrase.

=== edit-priority.md changes ===
1. Teach the Play to accept an OPTIONAL `details` field plus a `mode` for non-Objective body edits, with two modes (name them exactly):
   - `replace-body`: replace the body content that FOLLOWS the `## Objective` section with the provided `details` markdown VERBATIM, leaving the `## Objective` section (heading + approved objective text) and frontmatter unchanged.
   - `append-section`: append the provided `details` markdown VERBATIM as new trailing section(s) after the existing body, preserving everything above unchanged.
   In both modes the details are placed verbatim — no summarization/reordering/retitling.
2. State the Objective-preservation rule explicitly: a details body edit (`replace-body`/`append-section`) PRESERVES the existing `## Objective` by default; the Objective is only changed when the invocation explicitly carries the founder-approved new Objective (the existing founder-gated path). Keep the existing instruction that changing the Objective requires the explicit founder-approved objective.
3. PRESERVE these existing pinned phrases verbatim (a test asserts them): 'If the edit adds or changes implementation atoms', 'decision/taxonomy work before schema changes', 'proof/verification last' (the test matches /proof\/verification\s+last/), 'independently delegable', and 'elegance checkpoint'. Keep the round-trip validation via `parseFrontmatter` and `loadPriority`, and the no-git/commit-spine closing.

=== test pinning (keep the branch green AND pin the new owner) ===
4. In packages/core/tests/orchestration-contracts.test.ts, the existing test 'priority authoring surfaces derive markdown from the core priority composer' must still pass (it asserts the create-priority Play contains `composePriorityMarkdown` and does NOT restate the format). EXTEND it (or add a sibling assertion) so the create-priority Play now also contains the literal `composePriorityBody` — pinning the body-composer as the named owner. Do not weaken the existing not-restated assertions into tautologies.
5. In packages/core/tests/priority-authoring-plays.test.ts, add a focused assertion that edit-priority's body documents the verbatim detailed-body edit modes — assert it contains the literal `replace-body` and `append-section`. Keep all existing assertions in that file passing.

MUST NOT BREAK: every existing assertion in orchestration-contracts.test.ts and priority-authoring-plays.test.ts; the Plays must still load (loadEffectivePlay) as non-empty headless governance Plays with writeScope cocoder/priorities/**.

EXIT CRITERION (scripted): from repo root, `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core test` and `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core typecheck` both clean, including your new/extended assertions. Report the exact commands and output, and quote the final create-priority compose step and the two edit-priority mode descriptions you wrote. Do not run git.

data.reason: create-priority Play composes body via composePriorityBody then composePriorityMarkdown (verbatim details); edit-priority gains replace-body/append-section modes preserving Objective by default; pinned phrases intact; core typecheck clean and 611 tests pass incl. extended contract assertions.

data.task:
Ticket 0062, ATOM 4 of 5 — add the governed `cocoder oz edit-priority <id>` CLI wrapper so non-Objective body details (and founder-approved Objective changes) can be applied through the edit-priority authoring Play in one governed call. The edit-priority Play (atom 3) already understands a `details` field with `mode` = `replace-body` | `append-section`, preserves the Objective by default, and changes the Objective only when an explicit founder-approved `objective` is supplied. The daemon already routes the `edit-priority` playId generically and passes the invocation verbatim — so this atom is CLI-only. Mirror the existing create-priority command block.

WRITE SCOPE (strictly inside):
- packages/cli/src/oz-args.ts
- packages/cli/src/run.ts
- packages/cli/tests/oz-args.test.ts

WHAT TO BUILD:
1. FIRST, refactor to keep ONE owner of the details-source parsing. The `--details-file`/`--details-stdin` flag handling + rejection logic added to `createPriorityInvocation` in atom 2 must be extracted into a small shared helper in oz-args.ts (e.g. `resolveDetailsSource(args, deps): string | undefined`) that both `createPriorityInvocation` and the new `editPriorityInvocation` call. The helper owns: mutual-exclusivity (both flags → throw), missing `--details-file` path → throw, file read failure → clear throw, and returns the raw content (it does NOT decide emptiness — see below). Reuse the SAME `CreatePriorityInvocationDeps` reader-injection shape (rename to a neutral name like `DetailsSourceDeps` if cleaner; keep create-priority's exported type/behavior compatible). createPriorityInvocation must keep its current behavior and all its tests passing (empty/whitespace details still rejected with its existing message). Do NOT leave two copies of the details-source contract.
2. Add `editPriorityInvocation(args, deps?: DetailsSourceDeps)` to oz-args.ts. Contract:
   - `id`: the priority id, taken as the first positional arg (args[0]) — required, must be present and must not start with `--`; throw a clear Error if missing.
   - `--objective <text>`: OPTIONAL. When present and non-empty (trimmed), include `objective` in the invocation (this is the founder-approved Objective-change path). Empty/whitespace `--objective` value → throw.
   - `--mode <replace-body|append-section>`: governs how `details` applies.
   - `--details-file <path>` / `--details-stdin`: optional details source via the shared helper.
   - COUPLING RULES (throw clear Errors): if `details` is provided, `--mode` is REQUIRED and must be exactly `replace-body` or `append-section`; an invalid mode value → throw; `--mode` provided WITHOUT a details source → throw (mode needs details); details resolved to empty/whitespace → throw.
   - AT LEAST ONE meaningful edit required: if neither a details source NOR `--objective` is provided → throw (nothing to edit).
   - Returns an invocation object containing `id`, plus `details` and `mode` when details were provided, plus `objective` when provided. Details content is carried VERBATIM (no interior trimming). Omit keys that were not provided.
3. Wire run.ts: add a command block `if (cmd === 'oz' && arg1 === 'edit-priority')` mirroring the create-priority block (around packages/cli/src/run.ts:188-211): parse via `editPriorityInvocation(process.argv.slice(4), { readFileText: (p)=>readFileSync(p,'utf8'), readStdin: ()=>readFileSync(0,'utf8') })` inside try/catch that prints a new `editPriorityUsage` and exits 2 on parse error; probe the daemon and error out if not alive (same message style); call `authoringPlayViaDaemon(\`http://127.0.0.1:${live.port}\`, 'cocoder', 'edit-priority', invocation, 'oscar')`; print `edited priority ${invocation.id}: ${result.commitSha}` + committed files + turn log on success, mirroring create-priority's success/no-commit output.
4. Add `editPriorityUsage` and extend the top-level `usage` string to document: `cocoder oz edit-priority <id> [--objective <text>] [--mode <replace-body|append-section>] [--details-file <path> | --details-stdin]`.

TESTS (packages/cli/tests/oz-args.test.ts, match existing terse style):
- editPriorityInvocation: `<id> --mode replace-body --details-file x.md` with injected reader returning multi-section markdown → `{ id, mode:'replace-body', details:<verbatim> }`.
- `<id> --mode append-section --details-stdin` with injected stdin reader → `{ id, mode:'append-section', details }`.
- `<id> --objective "New approved objective"` only → `{ id, objective:'New approved objective' }` (no mode/details keys).
- `<id> --objective ... --mode replace-body --details-file x.md` → all of id/objective/mode/details present.
- rejections: missing id throws; details without --mode throws; --mode without details throws; invalid --mode value throws; both details sources throw (via shared helper); empty details throws; neither details nor objective throws (nothing to edit); empty --objective value throws.
- ALSO confirm the shared-helper refactor did not regress create-priority: keep all existing createPriorityInvocation tests passing unchanged.

MUST NOT BREAK: existing packages/cli tests; createPriorityInvocation public behavior; the daemon/Play (untouched).

EXIT CRITERION (scripted): from repo root, `pnpm --filter @cocoder/cli test` and `pnpm --filter @cocoder/cli typecheck` both clean, including new cases. Report exact commands + output. Do not run git.

data.reason: cocoder oz edit-priority <id> wrapper added (oz-args editPriorityInvocation + run.ts block + usage); shared resolveDetailsSource owns details parsing with create-priority behavior intact; modes/objective/coupling rejections covered; cli typecheck clean and 36 tests pass incl. 13 new.

data.task:
Ticket 0062, ATOM 5 of 5 (final) — add the daemon-level INTEGRATION PROOF that a create/edit-priority invocation carrying a detailed markdown `details` body flows through the authoring spine to a committed priority file with the Objective preserved. This closes the ticket's acceptance line 'Tests cover ... Objective preservation, loadPriority round-trip, committed file lists' for the GOVERNED path. (file/stdin parsing + malformed-input rejection are already covered by packages/cli/tests/oz-args.test.ts from atoms 2/4; core round-trip by packages/core/tests/priorities.test.ts from atom 1; the Objective-guard refusal cases already exist in this daemon test file at the 'refuses create-priority...' and 'refuses edit-priority when the edit blanks an existing Objective' tests — keep them green.)

WRITE SCOPE (strictly inside):
- packages/daemon/tests/authoring-play.test.ts

CONTEXT (read the existing file first): `requestAuthoringPlay` runs a headless turn (stubbed here via `makeFixture({ runHeadless })`) then commits the Play's write-scope through the governance spine. The stub MODELS what the prompt-only Play LLM writes. The real daemon code under test is: the invocation → prompt passthrough (`renderInvocation`/`buildAuthoringPlayPrompt`), the commit spine, and the Objective guard. The existing 'dispatches create-priority...' test (around line 55) is your template: it asserts `result.body.committedPaths`, that the prompt contains a stringified invocation field (e.g. `"objective": "Ship alpha."`), and uses `git(...)` + helpers like `writePriority`, `readPriorityOrder`. `@cocoder/core` is already imported in this file.

ADD TWO TESTS inside the `describe('requestAuthoringPlay', ...)` block:

1. 'create-priority carries a detailed body through the spine, preserving the Objective':
   - Define `const objective = 'Ship the detailed-body create path.'` and a multi-section `const details = ['## Phase A','','Badge: [details: exact]','','- Verification gate: load and prove details stay out of the Objective.','','## Non-goals','','- Do not summarize the detailed body.'].join('\n')`.
   - In `runHeadless`, compose the file the way the Play is documented to: import and use core `composePriorityBody({ objective, details })` and `composePriorityMarkdown({ id: 'alpha', title: 'Alpha', goal })`, and write it to `cocoder/priorities/alpha.md`. Return `{ exitCode: 0, output: 'created alpha' }`.
   - Invoke `requestAuthoringPlay` with `playId: 'create-priority'`, `invocation: { id: 'alpha', title: 'Alpha', objective, details }`.
   - Assert: `result.status === 200`; `result.body.committedPaths` equals `['cocoder/priorities/alpha.md', 'cocoder/priorities/order.json']`; `result.body.outOfLanePaths` is `[]`.
   - Assert PASSTHROUGH: `fixture.prompts[0]?.prompt` contains `composePriorityBody` is NOT what to check — instead assert it contains a distinctive verbatim details substring (e.g. `'Badge: [details: exact]'`) AND the JSON key `"details"`, proving the invocation's details reached the Play prompt via renderInvocation.
   - Assert COMMITTED CONTENT: `git(home, ['show', 'HEAD:cocoder/priorities/alpha.md'])` contains `'## Phase A'`, `'## Non-goals'`, and the verbatim badge/gate lines.
   - Assert OBJECTIVE PRESERVATION / round-trip: use core `loadPriority(join(home,'cocoder','priorities'),'alpha')` and expect `.objective` to equal exactly `objective` (NOT polluted by the details sections) and `.goal` to contain the `details` string.

2. 'edit-priority appends a detailed section while preserving the existing Objective':
   - Seed an existing committed priority: `await writePriority(home, 'alpha', 'Alpha', 'Keep the approved Objective.')` then git add+commit it (mirror the existing edit-priority test's seeding).
   - Define `const addendum = ['## Addendum','','New founder detail, verbatim.'].join('\n')`.
   - In `runHeadless`, model an `append-section` edit: write `cocoder/priorities/alpha.md` as the original frontmatter + `## Objective` + `Keep the approved Objective.` + a blank line + the `addendum` verbatim (you may compose via `composePriorityMarkdown({ id:'alpha', title:'Alpha', goal: composePriorityBody({ objective: 'Keep the approved Objective.', details: addendum }) })` since append-after-Objective on an Objective-only body is exactly that composition). Return `{ exitCode: 0, output: 'appended section' }`.
   - Invoke with `playId: 'edit-priority'`, `invocation: { id: 'alpha', mode: 'append-section', details: addendum }` (NO objective key — Objective must be preserved by default).
   - Assert: `result.status === 200`; `committedPaths` includes `'cocoder/priorities/alpha.md'`; HEAD commit subject is `governance: edit-priority` (via `git log -1 --pretty=%s`).
   - Assert PASSTHROUGH: prompt contains `"mode": "append-section"` and `"details"`.
   - Assert PRESERVATION: `git(home,['show','HEAD:cocoder/priorities/alpha.md'])` contains BOTH `'Keep the approved Objective.'` AND `'New founder detail, verbatim.'`; and `loadPriority(...).objective` still equals `'Keep the approved Objective.'`.

IMPORTS: add `loadPriority`, `composePriorityBody`, `composePriorityMarkdown` to the existing `@cocoder/core` import block as needed. Reuse the file's existing `git`, `writePriority`, `readPriorityOrder`, `makeFixture` helpers — do NOT duplicate them.

MUST NOT BREAK: every existing test in authoring-play.test.ts (and the wider daemon suite). Do not alter production code — this atom is tests only.

EXIT CRITERION (scripted): from repo root, `pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon test` and `pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon typecheck` both clean, including your two new tests. Report the exact commands and output, and quote the two new test names with their pass lines. Do not run git.
